content:
First, note that since \(\sum a_i=19\), if we set \(x_i=A_i-a_i\) then
\[
\sum x_i=\sum A_i-\sum a_i=19-19=0.
\]
Each \(A_i\) must be an integer, and because \(2.56\le a_i\le2.86\), the only reasonable choices are \(A_i=2\) or \(3\).  If \(A_i=2\) then \(x_i=2-a_i= -d_i\) where 
\[
d_i=a_i-2,
\]
and if \(A_i=3\) then \(x_i=3-a_i=+s_i\) where 
\[
s_i=3-a_i.
\]
We compute

i   ai  di=ai−2  si=3−ai  
1  2.56  0.56  0.44  
2  2.61  0.61  0.39  
3  2.65  0.65  0.35  
4  2.71  0.71  0.29  
5  2.79  0.79  0.21  
6  2.82  0.82  0.18  
7  2.86  0.86  0.14  

Since \(\sum x_i=0\), if we let \(k\) of the \(A_i\) be 3’s (and hence \(7-k\) be 2’s) one checks
\[
\sum x_i=\sum_{A_i=3}s_i-\sum_{A_i=2}d_i
=\bigl(\textstyle\sum_{A_i=3}(s_i+d_i)\bigr)-\sum d_i
=(k)-5.
\]
Hence we must have \(k=5\): exactly five of the \(A_i\) are 3 and two are 2.  To drive down the maximum error
\[
M=\max_i|A_i-a_i|=\max\bigl\{\max_{A_i=2}d_i,\;\max_{A_i=3}s_i\bigr\}
\]
we should pick the two smallest \(d_i\) to be the ones rounded down to 2, namely \(d_1=0.56\) and \(d_2=0.61\), and round the other five up (whose \(s_i\) are at most \(0.35\)).  Thus
\[
M=\max\{0.61,\;0.35\}=0.61,
\]
and
\[
100M=61.
\]
Answer: \(\boxed{61}\).

message.content:
First, note that since \(\sum a_i=19\), if we set \(x_i=A_i-a_i\) then
\[
\sum x_i=\sum A_i-\sum a_i=19-19=0.
\]
Each \(A_i\) must be an integer, and because \(2.56\le a_i\le2.86\), the only reasonable choices are \(A_i=2\) or \(3\).  If \(A_i=2\) then \(x_i=2-a_i= -d_i\) where 
\[
d_i=a_i-2,
\]
and if \(A_i=3\) then \(x_i=3-a_i=+s_i\) where 
\[
s_i=3-a_i.
\]
We compute

i   ai  di=ai−2  si=3−ai  
1  2.56  0.56  0.44  
2  2.61  0.61  0.39  
3  2.65  0.65  0.35  
4  2.71  0.71  0.29  
5  2.79  0.79  0.21  
6  2.82  0.82  0.18  
7  2.86  0.86  0.14  

Since \(\sum x_i=0\), if we let \(k\) of the \(A_i\) be 3’s (and hence \(7-k\) be 2’s) one checks
\[
\sum x_i=\sum_{A_i=3}s_i-\sum_{A_i=2}d_i
=\bigl(\textstyle\sum_{A_i=3}(s_i+d_i)\bigr)-\sum d_i
=(k)-5.
\]
Hence we must have \(k=5\): exactly five of the \(A_i\) are 3 and two are 2.  To drive down the maximum error
\[
M=\max_i|A_i-a_i|=\max\bigl\{\max_{A_i=2}d_i,\;\max_{A_i=3}s_i\bigr\}
\]
we should pick the two smallest \(d_i\) to be the ones rounded down to 2, namely \(d_1=0.56\) and \(d_2=0.61\), and round the other five up (whose \(s_i\) are at most \(0.35\)).  Thus
\[
M=\max\{0.61,\;0.35\}=0.61,
\]
and
\[
100M=61.
\]
Answer: \(\boxed{61}\).
